html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太上立德，次立功，次立言 - Classic triad of values: 'The highest is to establish virtue, next is to establish merit, and next is to establish words.' Reflects the Confucian hierarchy of moral, practical, and intellectual achievements.
  • 言之不朽，与德功并 - Means 'Words achieve immortality, on par with virtue and merit,' emphasizing the enduring value of written works.
  • 抑奚分乎穷达显晦哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How can one distinguish between those who are poor and obscure or rich and famous?' Challenges the idea that fame or obscurity affects the lasting value of one's words.
  • 达而在上，名显，则言因之而显；穷而在下，名晦，则言亦因之而晦 - Explains that when one is successful and famous, their words gain prominence, but when they are poor and obscure, their words also fade into obscurity.
  • 顾显于今者，未必其显于后也；晦于始者，未必其晦于终也 - Counterpoint to the previous statement, suggesting that what is famous now may not remain so in the future, and what is obscure at the start may gain recognition later.
  • 古今来大手笔，一代不过数人 - Means 'Throughout history, great writers have been few in each generation,' highlighting the rarity of truly significant literary figures.
  • 人不必皆登廊庙 - Means 'Not everyone needs to enter the halls of government,' suggesting that literary achievement does not require political power.
  • 骛名之士沉浸簿书，荒废笔墨 - Describes those who pursue fame by immersing themselves in bureaucratic paperwork and neglecting their writing.
  • 恬不为怪 - Means 'They take it without surprise,' indicating that such behavior is accepted as normal.
  • 夫岂若跧伏里巷者之犹得以言警世也耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How is this better than those who live in the alleys and still use their words to warn the world?' Contrasts the value of writing by ordinary people with that of those in power.
  • 吾友叔平，固长于立言者也 - Means 'My friend Shuping is indeed skilled in establishing words (or writings),' praising the subject's literary abilities.
  • 兰苕馆撰述宏富，堪继龙眠诸先辈之后 - Describes Shuping's extensive writings, which are comparable to those of earlier masters from Longmen (a famous literary region).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 廊庙 - Refers to the halls of government, symbolizing political power and authority.
  • 簿书 - Refers to official documents or bureaucratic paperwork, representing the administrative duties of government officials.
  • 兰苕馆 - Likely refers to a literary salon or study where Shuping worked, named after a place or person associated with literature.
  • 龙眠 - Refers to Longmen, a famous literary region in Chinese history, known for producing many great writers and scholars.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The triadic structure of '太上立德，次立功，次立言' must be translated in a way that preserves the hierarchical relationship between the three.
  • The parallel structure of '达而在上，名显，则言因之而显；穷而在下，名晦，则言亦因之而晦' must be translated in a way that maintains the balance between the two clauses.
  • The counterpoint structure of '顾显于今者，未必其显于后也；晦于始者，未必其晦于终也' must be translated in a way that preserves the rhetorical force of the contrast.
  • The sentence '古今来大手笔，一代不过数人，人不必皆登廊庙' combines a general statement about the rarity of great writers with a specific point about their lack of need for political power, and it must be translated in a way that preserves the logical flow.
  • The complex sentence '骛名之士沉浸簿书，荒废笔墨，役役于富贵之场，问其何德？何功？人皆匿笑之，而恬不为怪' describes the behavior of those who pursue fame through bureaucracy and neglects their writing, and it must be translated in a way that preserves the criticism of such behavior.
  • The rhetorical question '夫岂若跧伏里巷者之犹得以言警世也耶' contrasts the value of writing by ordinary people with that of those in power, and it must be translated in a way that preserves the rhetorical force of the contrast.

Proper Nouns

  • 太上 - Refers to the highest level in the triad of values.
  • 叔平 - The name of the author's friend, Shuping.
  • 兰苕馆 - The name of the literary salon or study where Shuping worked.
  • 龙眠 - Refers to Longmen, a famous literary region in Chinese history.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃侘傺无聊 - Classical expression meaning feeling dejected and aimless, often used to describe a state of disappointment or frustration.
  • 抱璞屡刖 - Literally means holding the uncarved jade and being repeatedly cut, a metaphor for enduring hardship or rejection despite possessing talent or virtue.
  • 佣书至老 - Means working as a copyist until old age, indicating a life of humble labor.
  • 捧檄未能 - Refers to the inability to respond to a summons or call to duty, often used to express a sense of failure or missed opportunity.
  • 因慨然举平日所记载以备劝惩者 - Means thus, with a sense of resolve, he collected his daily records to serve as examples for encouragement and punishment.
  • 汇为一编 - Means compiled into a single volume.
  • 署曰《里乘》 - Means titled it *The Local Chronicles*.
  • 丐序于予 - Means asked me to write a preface.
  • 将付之梓 - Means planned to have it printed.
  • 予维叔平才优学博 - Means I consider Shu Ping to be talented and well-learned.
  • 其帖括力宗先正 - Means his essay-writing style is firmly rooted in the teachings of the ancients.
  • 尤足挽回风气，为士林模范 - Means especially capable of reviving the风气and serving as a model for scholars.
  • 脱令致身通显，达而在上 - Means if he were to achieve high office and prominence.
  • 度必大有所建白 - Means he would surely make significant contributions.
  • 为桑梓光 - Means bring glory to his hometown.
  • 何天厄其遇，而使之偃蹇半生 - Means Why has fate been so harsh, making him struggle for half his life?
  • 俾以著书立说消磨岁月焉 - Means forcing him to pass the years through writing and establishing doctrines.
  • 将见此编出，而人之流览者，懔然于福善禂淫之理 - Means When this volume is published, those who read it will be deeply aware of the principles of virtue and vice.
  • 晓然于惠迪从逆之机 - Means clearly understand the mechanisms of obedience and rebellion.
  • 以口舌代木铎 - Means using words to replace the wooden bell, a metaphor for spreading moral teachings.
  • 世道人心，关系实非浅鲜 - Means the world's morals and people's hearts are truly greatly affected.
  • 则虽小说家言，作董狐观可也 - Means even if it is a work of fiction, it can be read as a truthful account like that of Dang Hu.
  • 叔平传已 - Means The biography of Shu Ping is complete.
  • 同治十有三年九月既望 - Means the 13th year of Tongzhi, on the 15th day of the ninth month.
  • 定远子箴方濬颐撰 - Means written by Dingyuan Zi Zhen Fang Junyi.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 叔平 - The name of the subject of the biography, Shu Ping.
  • 《里乘》 - The title of the work being discussed, *The Local Chronicles*.
  • 帖括 - A term referring to the style of writing for imperial examinations.
  • 先正 - Refers to the revered masters of the past, particularly in the context of literary and moral teachings.
  • 桑梓 - A metaphor for one's hometown or native place.
  • 董狐 - A legendary historian from the Spring and Autumn period known for his impartial and truthful records.
  • 同治 - The era name of the Tongzhi Emperor, who ruled from 1862 to 1874.
  • 定远子箴方濬颐 - The name of the author of the preface, Fang Junyi, with '定远子' being his courtesy name.

Proper Nouns

  • 叔平 - The subject of the biography.
  • 《里乘》 - The title of the work.
  • 帖括 - A term related to imperial examination writing.
  • 先正 - Refers to revered masters of the past.
  • 桑梓 - A metaphor for one's hometown.
  • 董狐 - A legendary historian.
  • 同治 - The era name of the Tongzhi Emperor.
  • 定远子箴方濬颐 - The name of the author of the preface.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 桐城 - A city in Anhui province, known for its rich cultural heritage and literary tradition.
  • 戊辰 - A traditional Chinese calendar year, corresponding to 1898 in the Gregorian calendar.
  • 權臬皖垣 - Acting as the governor of Anhui, where '臬' refers to the governor, and '皖垣' is a poetic way of referring to Anhui province.
  • 一見傾談 - A conversation that flows easily and deeply upon first meeting, suggesting a strong connection.
  • 真率無飾 - Sincere and unadorned, describing a person's character.
  • 恂恂然書生本色 - Gentle and scholarly in nature, emphasizing the person's academic background.
  • 可親可敬 - Approachable and respectable, highlighting the person's qualities.
  • 望而知為有道士也 - One can see that he has a scholarly and virtuous demeanor, implying a deep respect for learning and virtue.
  • 既罄讀蘭苕館所著十余種 - After reading all the works published by LanTiao Pavilion, indicating a thorough examination of the author's publications.
  • 中有《里乘》十卷 - Among them, there are ten volumes of 'Li Cheng', referring to a specific work.
  • 勸善書也 - A book that encourages moral and ethical behavior, describing the purpose of the work.
  • 予嘗謂 - I have often said, introducing a personal opinion.
  • 如嘗人以異味 - Like offering someone a rare delicacy, using a metaphor to describe the experience of being encouraged to do good.
  • 必調劑五味 - One must adjust the five flavors, referring to the careful preparation of food.
  • 烹飪得法 - Cooking it properly, emphasizing the importance of technique.
  • 使人食而甘之 - Making the person enjoy it, indicating the desired outcome.
  • 欲輟箸而不舍 - Wishing to stop eating but unable to, suggesting a strong desire to continue.
  • 又如導人以佳境 - Like guiding someone to a beautiful place, using another metaphor to describe the experience.
  • 必道路幽折 - The path must be winding and secluded, describing the journey.
  • 疆界明廠 - The boundaries must be clear and well-defined, emphasizing clarity.
  • 數武而亭，數武而榭 - Every few steps there is a pavilion, every few steps there is a terrace, describing the layout of the place.
  • 又數武而樓、而台、而軒、而閣、而洞房、而精舍 - Every few steps there is a tower, a platform, a pavilion, a hall, a cave room, and a study, further detailing the layout.
  • 豆籬、瓜圃、菜畦、禾畮 - Bean trellises, melon gardens, vegetable plots, and rice fields, describing the agricultural elements.
  • 妥貼玲瓏 - Graceful and delicate, describing the aesthetic quality.
  • 相地設施 - Arranging according to the terrain, emphasizing practicality.
  • 各極其妙 - Each reaching its utmost perfection, indicating the highest level of craftsmanship.
  • 其中奇峰曲沼、流水小橋、花草竹木、禽獸蟲魚之屬 - Among which there are strange peaks, winding ponds, flowing streams, small bridges, flowers, grass, bamboo, trees, birds, animals, insects, and fish, describing the natural beauty.
  • 靡不畢備 - Nothing is missing, emphasizing completeness.
  • 所在引人入勝 - Everywhere attracting people to admire, indicating the appeal of the place.
  • 樂而忘疲 - Enjoying it so much that one forgets fatigue, describing the immersive experience.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蘭苕館 - A publishing house or literary salon, likely associated with the author Xu Jun Shuping.
  • 《里乘》十卷 - A specific work by Xu Jun Shuping, consisting of ten volumes.

Proper Nouns

  • 桐城許君叔平 - Xu Jun Shuping from Tongcheng, where '桐城' is a place name, and '許君叔平' is the name of a person.
  • 戊辰 - A traditional Chinese calendar year, corresponding to 1898 in the Gregorian calendar.
  • 權臬皖垣 - Acting as the governor of Anhui, where '臬' refers to the governor, and '皖垣' is a poetic way of referring to Anhui province.
  • 蘭苕館 - A publishing house or literary salon, likely associated with the author Xu Jun Shuping.
  • 《里乘》十卷 - A specific work by Xu Jun Shuping, consisting of ten volumes.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 劝人为善 - Means 'to encourage others to do good,' a common Confucian ethical theme.
  • 如斯二者而已 - Means 'it is nothing more than these two things,' suggesting a limited scope.
  • 善书至今亦甚夥矣 - Means 'books on goodness have been numerous up to this day,' indicating a long tradition.
  • 或尚典奥，村氓懵然不知 - Means 'some are too scholarly and obscure, so that even village folks are bewildered,' criticizing overly complex texts.
  • 或尚鄙俚，学士哑然不屑 - Means 'some are too vulgar and crude, so that scholars are silent and disdainful,' criticizing overly simplistic texts.
  • 君有鉴于其失 - Means 'you have taken note of their shortcomings,' suggesting an awareness of problems.
  • 埽去陈言 - Means 'to sweep away old clichés,' indicating a desire for innovation.
  • 蒐辑新事 - Means 'to gather new stories,' emphasizing the collection of fresh material.
  • 信手拈来 - Means 'to pick up effortlessly,' describing ease in writing.
  • 雅俗共喻 - Means 'understood by both the refined and the common people,' indicating accessibility.
  • 生公说法 - Refers to the story of Sengzhao, a famous Buddhist monk known for his eloquent preaching, used here to mean 'preaching that convinces everyone.'
  • 必使人人点头而后已 - Means 'to make everyone nod in agreement before stopping,' emphasizing persuasive power.
  • 可贵也 - Means 'is valuable,' summarizing the importance of the work.
  • 顾君博览群书，著作等身 - Means 'you have read widely and written extensively,' praising the author's erudition.
  • 壹皆可传于世 - Means 'all can be passed down to future generations,' indicating the lasting value of the works.
  • 欿然不敢自信 - Means 'modestly lacking confidence,' suggesting humility despite achievements.
  • 间与论政治得失 - Means 'occasionally discussing political gains and losses,' indicating involvement in political discourse.
  • 多精创不敷 - Means 'often being innovative and insightful,' praising the quality of the discussions.
  • 而久困名场 - Means 'but long trapped in the world of fame,' suggesting frustration with the pursuit of recognition.
  • 仅以从征功荐擢邑令 - Means 'only promoted to a county magistrate through military merit,' indicating a modest official position.
  • 谒选绌资 - Means 'failing to secure a position through selection,' suggesting disappointment in career advancement.
  • 天亦何靳其言 - Means 'why does heaven withhold its words,' questioning divine favor.
  • 不使及身一试耶 - Means 'not allowing him to try once in his lifetime,' expressing frustration with missed opportunities.
  • 予尝撰楹联赠之 - Means 'I once wrote a couplet to give to him,' indicating a personal gesture.
  • 于此间得真名士 - Means 'to find a true scholar here,' praising the person's qualities.
  • 愿他日为贤有司 - Means 'to hope he will become a virtuous official one day,' expressing a wish for future success.
  • 知君者，当不以予言为阿好也 - Means 'those who know you will not think my words are flattering,' indicating sincerity.
  • 即不知君者，见君此集，可想见其为人 - Means 'even those who do not know you, seeing your collection, can imagine what kind of person you are,' suggesting the work reflects the author's character.
  • 亦当不以予言为阿好也哉 - Means 'will also not think my words are flattering,' reinforcing the sincerity of the praise.
  • 大梁南卿刘毓楠拜撰 - Means 'Duan Nanying of Daliang respectfully wrote,' providing the author's name and location.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 劝人为善 - A central Confucian ethical principle.
  • 善书 - Refers to books on moral conduct and goodness, a significant genre in Chinese literature.
  • 生公说法 - Refers to the story of Sengzhao, a famous Buddhist monk known for his eloquent preaching, used here to mean 'preaching that convinces everyone.'
  • 名场 - Refers to the competitive world of fame and recognition, often associated with the imperial examination system.
  • 从征功荐擢邑令 - Refers to the practice of promoting officials based on military merit, a common method of advancement in imperial China.
  • 谒选绌资 - Refers to the process of selecting officials, where failure to secure a position can be a source of disappointment.
  • 楹联 - Refers to couplets, a form of Chinese poetry often used for gifts and decorations.
  • 大梁南卿 - Refers to a place and title, likely indicating a specific region and rank.
  • 刘毓楠 - The name of the author, providing a historical figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 劝人为善 - The central ethical theme.
  • 善书 - Books on moral conduct and goodness.
  • 生公说法 - The story of Sengzhao, a famous Buddhist monk.
  • 名场 - The competitive world of fame and recognition.
  • 从征功荐擢邑令 - Promotion based on military merit.
  • 谒选绌资 - The process of selecting officials.
  • 楹联 - Couplets, a form of Chinese poetry.
  • 大梁南卿 - A place and title.
  • 刘毓楠 - The name of the author.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明镜烛景，妍媸毕呈 - A mirror that illuminates all scenes, revealing both beauty and ugliness.
  • 灵犀劈流，清浊攸判 - A sharp mind that divides clear from turbid waters.
  • 言者无罪，臧否罔敢 - Those who speak have no fault, but it is not dared to criticize.
  • 和风煦物，见恺泽之襟怀 - Gentle breezes warm things, showing a benevolent heart.
  • 庆云缦霄，真吉祥之文字 - Auspicious clouds stretch across the sky, truly auspicious words.
  • 名山不朽，寿世无疑矣 - Immortalized on famous mountains, its longevity is beyond doubt.
  • 刻划怨旷，组织因缘 - Carving out grievances and organizing causes.
  • 东墙窥臣，西厢背母 - Peeping from the east wall and betraying one's mother in the west wing.
  • 盟要啮臂，叙闺阁之幽情 - Swearing by biting arms, narrating the hidden emotions of women.
  • 事胜画眉，绘床帷之媟态 - Actions surpass painting eyebrows, depicting the intimate scenes of beds and curtains.
  • 狂荡鲜耻，其弊也亵 - Rash and shameless, their fault is impropriety.
  • 屈指英雄，倾心任侠 - Counting heroes on fingers,倾心于侠客.
  • 把臂伏莽，吹唇揭竿 - Holding arms in the wilderness, blowing lips and raising flags.
  • 智远韬名，牛角挂书之辈 - Smart and hiding their names, those who hang books on cow horns.
  • 扶馀创业，虬髯得意之秋 - Founding a dynasty like Fuyu, the time when a dragon's whiskers are pleased.
  • 犷悍藐法，其弊也横 - Wild and lawless, their fault is violence.
  • 设森罗之恶狱，造纣绝之幽宫 - Setting up evil prisons in the underworld, creating dark palaces of King Zhou.
  • 袭左氏之豕人，述阿尼之猫鬼 - Imitating *Zuo Zhuan*'s pig people, narrating Ani's cat ghosts.
  • 野狐拜月，影幢幢而悸心 - Wild foxes worshiping the moon, shadows making hearts tremble.
  • 山魈吟风，声霄霄而竖发 - Mountain demons singing in the wind, sounds making hair stand on end.
  • 离奇变怪，其弊也诞 - Strange and bizarre, their fault is absurdity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《里乘》 - A fictional or historical work, possibly a local chronicle or novel.
  • 桐城 - A place name, likely referring to Tongcheng, a city in Anhui Province known for its literary tradition.
  • 叔平先生 - A respectful title for a scholar or writer.
  • 明镜烛景 - A metaphor for a clear and revealing perspective.
  • 灵犀 - A metaphor for a sharp and perceptive mind.
  • 和风煦物 - A metaphor for gentle and benevolent influence.
  • 庆云缦霄 - A metaphor for auspicious and beautiful phenomena.
  • 名山不朽 - A metaphor for lasting fame and recognition.
  • 小说家言 - Refers to the genre of novels or fictional works.
  • 刻划怨旷 - Refers to the portrayal of grievances and longings.
  • 组织因缘 - Refers to the arrangement of causes and effects.
  • 东墙窥臣，西厢背母 - Refers to specific narrative elements or themes in literature.
  • 盟要啮臂 - Refers to a ritual of swearing oaths.
  • 叙闺阁之幽情 - Refers to the depiction of women's inner feelings.
  • 事胜画眉 - Refers to actions that surpass mere appearance.
  • 绘床帷之媟态 - Refers to the depiction of intimate scenes.
  • 狂荡鲜耻 - Refers to a lack of moral restraint.
  • 屈指英雄 - Refers to the enumeration of heroes.
  • 倾心任侠 - Refers to a preference for chivalrous behavior.
  • 把臂伏莽 - Refers to hiding in the wilderness.
  • 吹唇揭竿 - Refers to raising a rebellion.
  • 智远韬名 - Refers to intelligence and discretion.
  • 牛角挂书 - Refers to a humble and studious lifestyle.
  • 扶馀创业 - Refers to founding a dynasty.
  • 虬髯得意之秋 - Refers to a time of success and satisfaction.
  • 犷悍藐法 - Refers to a disregard for law and order.
  • 设森罗之恶狱 - Refers to the creation of hellish places.
  • 造纣绝之幽宫 - Refers to the construction of dark and forbidden palaces.
  • 袭左氏之豕人 - Refers to the imitation of *Zuo Zhuan*'s characters.
  • 述阿尼之猫鬼 - Refers to the narration of supernatural beings.
  • 野狐拜月 - Refers to a supernatural or mythological scene.
  • 山魈吟风 - Refers to a supernatural or mythological scene.
  • 离奇变怪 - Refers to strange and unusual events.
  • 诞 - Refers to absurdity or exaggeration.

Proper Nouns

  • 《里乘》 - The Village Chronicles.
  • 桐城 - Tongcheng.
  • 叔平先生 - Mr. Shuping.
  • 左氏 - Zuo Zhuan (The Commentary of Zuo).
  • 阿尼 - Ani (possibly a reference to a specific character or story).

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 拈花呈佛 - Buddhist metaphor meaning 'to present a flower to the Buddha,' symbolizing the transmission of enlightenment.
  • 采药求仙 - Means 'to gather herbs in search of immortality,' referring to alchemical practices and the pursuit of longevity.
  • 宝筏回头 - Metaphor from Buddhist scripture meaning 'to turn back to the precious raft,' symbolizing returning to the path of enlightenment.
  • 金丹换骨 - Refers to the alchemical process of transforming the body through the consumption of 'golden elixir' to achieve immortality.
  • 五百道小夫人之乳 - Metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the abundance of spiritual nourishment or the many paths of enlightenment.
  • 四百门大昆仑之城 - Metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the vast and mysterious realm of the Kunlun Mountains, associated with immortality and the divine.
  • 渺茫恍惚 - Means 'vague and hazy,' describing a state of uncertainty or ambiguity.
  • 其弊也荒 - Means 'its drawback is that it becomes wild or uncontrolled.'
  • 净祓四弊 - Means 'to cleanse and eliminate four faults,' referring to the purification of certain vices or errors.
  • 兼具三长 - Means 'to possess three virtues or strengths,' referring to a balanced and virtuous character.
  • 根柢六经 - Means 'to have the fundamentals rooted in the Six Classics,' referring to a deep understanding of classical texts.
  • 炉冶百子 - Means 'to refine and forge a hundred schools of thought,' referring to the synthesis of diverse intellectual traditions.
  • 实事求是 - Means 'to seek truth from facts,' emphasizing the importance of empirical evidence.
  • 祖《麟经》之义严 - Refers to adhering to the strict principles of the *Lunyu* (Analects), a key Confucian text.
  • 修辞立诚 - Means 'to establish sincerity through rhetoric,' emphasizing the importance of truthful expression.
  • 效狐史之笔直 - Refers to emulating the straightforward and honest style of the *Huxi* (Historical Records), a classic historical text.
  • 侯其炜而蔑以加矣 - Means 'to be splendid and unmatched,' describing something that is truly outstanding.
  • 居龙眠人文之薮 - Means 'to reside in a fertile ground of human culture,' referring to a place rich in intellectual and cultural activity.
  • 擅马迁叙述之才 - Means 'to possess the narrative talent of Sima Qian,' referring to the author of the *Historical Records*.
  • 策名秘苑 - Means 'to be listed in the imperial archives,' referring to being recognized by the court.
  • 儤直清班 - Means 'to serve in a clean and upright office,' referring to holding a position of integrity.
  • 花砖昼趋 - Metaphor for the bustling activity of the imperial court during the day.
  • 莲烛宵跋 - Metaphor for the continuation of work into the night, symbolized by lotus candles.
  • 制作必空馀子 - Means 'to create works that surpass others,' referring to the excellence of one's creations.
  • 声誉迥轶恒流 - Means 'to have a reputation that far exceeds the ordinary,' referring to a distinguished and exceptional standing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 拈花呈佛 - Reference to the Buddhist tradition of the transmission of the Dharma, often associated with the story of Buddha and Mahakasyapa.

  • 效狐史之笔直 - Refers to the straightforward and honest style of the *Huxi* (Historical Records), a classic historical text attributed to Sima Qian.
  • 侯其炜而蔑以加矣 - Refers to the Confucian ideal of achieving excellence and being unmatched in one's field.
  • 居龙眠人文之薮 - Refers to a place rich in intellectual and cultural activity, often associated with the Confucian ideal of a fertile ground for learning.

  • 声誉迥轶恒流 - Refers to the Confucian ideal of having a reputation that far exceeds the ordinary, emphasizing the importance of distinction and excellence.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 《里乘》一书最后出 - The phrase '最后出' (lastly appeared) is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'appeared last' or 'was published last.'
  • 身际乱离，目击因果 - This phrase is elliptical and could be read as 'experiencing turmoil and witnessing the effects of cause and effect.'
  • 文心结构如剥蕉抽茧 - This metaphorical phrase compares the structure of the text to peeling a banana and unwinding silk, suggesting a gradual and detailed process.
  • 绘声绘影，无不毕现纸上 - This phrase is elliptical and could be read as 'describing vividly and realistically, everything is fully displayed on paper.'
  • 使阅者欣然喜，憬然悟，终之以凛然惧 - This phrase is a series of participial clauses, each describing a different emotional response to the text.
  • 先生教世之心若是，其明且切也 - This phrase is elliptical and could be read as 'the teacher's intention to educate is so clear and direct.'
  • 可谓尽有小说家之长而祛其短，足与正史相表里者矣 - This phrase is a complex sentence with multiple clauses, each describing different aspects of the book.
  • 余识先生于题襟馆中数年 - This phrase is elliptical and could be read as 'I met Mr. Xu Shuping at the *Tijin* Hall for several years.'
  • 今夏再游邗江，出以见示，盖已付剞劂，公诸同好矣 - This phrase is a series of participial clauses, each describing a different action.
  • 亟跋数语，使海内有心人读之，勿徒为《搜神》、《齐谐》观也 - This phrase is a complex sentence with multiple clauses, each describing a different purpose of the translator's note.
  • 先生年已六十，叠举二雄，天之报施，于此可见 - This phrase is elliptical and could be read as 'Mr. Xu Shuping is now sixty years old, having achieved two great deeds, the retribution of heaven can be seen here.'
  • 视汤若士地下之《牡丹亭》，其用心不大判乎 - This phrase is a comparison, suggesting that Mr. Xu Shuping's work is similar in intention to *The Peony Pavilion*.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岁时伏腊 - Refers to traditional Chinese festivals, particularly the winter solstice and summer solstice, which were times of communal feasting and religious observance.
  • 报赛爨弄 - Involves rituals of gratitude to deities and ancestors, often accompanied by communal meals and performances.
  • 忠臣、孝子、仁人、正士 - These are archetypal figures in Chinese moral and literary tradition, representing loyalty, filial piety, benevolence, and integrity.
  • 权奸忤逆、佥壬宵小 - Refers to corrupt officials, traitors, and lowly villains, who are often portrayed as antagonists in Chinese literature.
  • 福善祸淫 - A Confucian and Buddhist principle that emphasizes the moral order of the universe, where good deeds are rewarded and evil deeds are punished.
  • 农工之暇 - Reflects the agrarian and manual labor-based society of traditional China, where leisure time was often spent in communal activities.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening phrase "岁时伏腊，报赛爨弄" introduces the context of seasonal festivals and communal rituals.
  • The clause "遇演忠臣、孝子、仁人、正士，无不肃然起敬，津津称叹者" describes the reactions of people when they encounter performances of virtuous characters.
  • The clause "遇演权奸忤逆、佥壬宵小，无不决眥恚愤，交口唾骂者" contrasts the reactions to performances of villainous characters.
  • The extended clause "甚至演生天成佛、及地狱种种变相，又无不羡怖交集，以为福善祸淫，报施不爽，而互相劝戒不置者" describes the reactions to performances of Buddhist themes, emphasizing the moral lessons derived from them.
  • The clause "于以见人心好恶之公，而秉彝之未泯也" summarizes the moral lessons learned from these performances, emphasizing the shared human sense of right and wrong.
  • The clause "其或农工之暇，二三野老，晚饭杯酒" introduces the setting of rural leisure time, where villagers gather to socialize.
  • The phrase "暑则豆棚瓜架，寒则地炉活火" describes the different settings for social gatherings in summer and winter.
  • The long clause "促膝言欢，论今评古，穷原竟委，影响傅会、邪正善恶、是非曲直，居然凿凿可据" describes the topics of conversation among the villagers, emphasizing their depth and clarity.
  • The clause "一时妇孺环听，不自知其手舞足蹈" describes the enthusiastic reactions of the audience to the villagers' discussions.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鬼神之德之盛 - Classical expression emphasizing the greatness of the virtues of ghosts and spirits.

  • 发乎情，止乎礼义 - Means that emotions should be expressed within the bounds of propriety.
  • 逾墙钻穴、狂荡苟且之事，概不录焉 - Refers to actions like climbing walls and digging holes, which are reckless and improper, and should not be recorded.
  • 说部所载淫情媟状，类多凭空结撰，尤伤心术 - Suggests that stories of sexual misconduct in literature are often fabricated and particularly harmful to moral values.
  • 绿林之徒，法所不贷 - Indicates that outlaws are not spared by the law.
  • 以其所言所行盗也，而几近于道矣 - Suggests that even though they are thieves, their words and actions are close to the right path.
  • 葑菲蒭荛，皆有可采，阅者亦当以为然也 - Means that even humble and common things have value and readers should agree with this perspective.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鬼神 - Refers to ghosts and spirits, which play a significant role in traditional Chinese beliefs.
  • 先师 - Refers to the teacher or mentor who has imparted wisdom.
  • 雷部 - Refers to the Thunder Department, a group of deities in Chinese mythology associated with thunder and lightning.
  • 狐仙 - Refers to fox spirits, which are often depicted in Chinese folklore as having magical powers and human-like qualities.
  • 闺阁儿女私情 - Refers to romantic entanglements among women and men, often depicted in literature.
  • 绿林之徒 - Refers to outlaws or bandits, a term often used in historical contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence introduces the topic of the virtues of ghosts and spirits and the teacher's repeated emphasis on it.
  • This clause describes the consequences of harmful actions and the severe punishments in the afterlife.
  • This clause introduces the Thunder Department as the most powerful and visible gods.
  • This clause contrasts the fabricated nature of fox spirit stories in literature with the reality of real events.
  • This clause describes the detailed depiction of romantic entanglements and their potential harm to social norms.
  • This clause emphasizes the severity of the law against outlaws.
  • This clause concludes by emphasizing the value of humble and common things and the reader's agreement with this perspective.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾儒出膺民社 - 吾儒 refers to 'we Confucians' or 'we scholars,' and 出膺民社 means 'to be appointed to serve as local officials.' This phrase indicates the role of Confucian scholars in governance.
  • 听讼最难 - 听讼 means 'to hear lawsuits' or 'to adjudicate disputes,' and 最难 means 'most difficult.' This phrase highlights the complexity of judicial duties.
  • 如遇人命所关 - 人命所关 means 'concerning human life,' emphasizing the gravity of cases involving life and death.
  • 慎而益慎 - 慎 means 'cautious,' and 益慎 means 'even more cautious.' This phrase stresses the need for extreme care in handling serious cases.
  • 辄笔之，另为一卷 - 辄笔之 means 'to write it down immediately,' and 另为一卷 means 'to compile it into a separate volume.' This indicates the practice of recording significant cases.
  • 为民父母者，其留意焉 - 为民父母者 means 'those who serve as parents to the people' (i.e., local officials), and 其留意焉 means 'please pay attention to it.' This is a call for officials to be mindful of the lessons in the recorded cases.
  • 述而不作，先师且然 - 述而不作 means 'to narrate without creating,' and 先师且然 means 'even our revered teachers did so.' This reflects the Confucian tradition of transmitting knowledge rather than inventing new ideas.
  • 丛书秘册，与故老遗编 - 丛书秘册 means 'collected books and secret scrolls,' and 故老遗编 means 'old writings left behind by the wise.' These terms refer to valuable and rare texts.
  • 删繁就简 - 删繁 means 'to remove complexity,' and 就简 means 'to simplify.' This phrase describes the process of editing texts.
  • 全录其文 - 全录 means 'to copy entirely,' and 其文 means 'the text.' This indicates the practice of reproducing texts in full.
  • 汇成一卷 - 汇成 means 'to compile,' and 一卷 means 'a single volume.' This phrase refers to the compilation of texts into a cohesive work.
  • 必标出作者姓名，以不敢掠美也 - 必标出 means 'must be marked,' 作者姓名 means 'author's name,' and 以不敢掠美也 means 'so as not to claim credit for others' work.' This emphasizes the importance of acknowledging sources.
  • 粤寇之乱 - 粤寇 refers to 'Guangdong bandits' or 'Wokou' (Japanese pirates), and 之乱 means 'the rebellion.' This phrase refers to the Wokou invasions during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 十馀年来生灵涂炭极矣 - 十馀年 means 'more than ten years,' 生灵涂炭 means 'the people are suffering greatly,' and 极矣 means 'to the extreme.' This phrase describes the severe suffering caused by the Wokou invasions.
  • 流离转徙，间关数万里 - 流离转徙 means 'to wander and relocate,' and 间关数万里 means 'to travel thousands of miles.' This phrase describes the hardships of migration.
  • 辄笔志之 - 辄笔 means 'to write down immediately,' and 志之 means 'to record it.' This indicates the practice of documenting personal experiences.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我不预闻，于我无尤也 - Formal expression meaning 'If I am not informed beforehand, I have no fault in it.' Reflects detachment from responsibility when not involved in a matter.
  • 我闻之，固不必谏劝阻止 - Means 'If I hear of it, I certainly do not need to advise, dissuade, or stop it.' Conveys a refusal to interfere in a situation.
  • 以逆彼之听 - Means 'to go against his hearing,' implying an attempt to contradict or oppose someone's decision or opinion.
  • 又何必撺掇怂恿，以助彼之虐乎 - Means 'why should I incite or encourage him to aid his cruelty?' Expresses a refusal to support or enable harmful actions.
  • 某翁洞悉人情 - Means 'a certain old man deeply understands human nature,' indicating wisdom and insight into human behavior.
  • 窥某甲怒气方张 - Means 'observing that Mr. A's anger was just beginning to rise,' describing a moment of escalating emotion.
  • 愿假刀以利其用 - Means 'willing to lend a knife to sharpen his use,' a metaphor for providing tools or means to someone, often with a negative connotation.
  • 又劝饮以壮其胆 - Means 'and to encourage drinking to bolster his courage,' suggesting the use of alcohol to embolden someone.
  • 语语中肯 - Means 'every word was to the point,' indicating that the old man's words were highly effective and appropriate.
  • 甲焉得不堕其术中 - Means 'how could Mr. A not fall into his scheme?' Highlights the inevitability of Mr. A being manipulated.
  • 托如厕，悄往告妇 - Means 'pretending to go to the toilet, he secretly went to inform the wife,' describing a deceptive action.
  • 寓谏劝阻止于撺掇怂恿之中 - Means 'to embed advice, dissuasion, and prevention within incitement and encouragement,' a complex phrase indicating the old man's subtle strategy.
  • 斟酌可谓尽善矣 - Means 'his consideration can be said to be perfect,' praising the old man's careful and thoughtful approach.
  • 及甲恨而返叩 - Means 'when Mr. A returned in anger,' describing a moment of confrontation.
  • 即直承不讳 - Means 'he immediately confessed without reservation,' indicating honesty and openness.
  • 并为剖陈利害 - Means 'and explained the benefits and harms,' suggesting a detailed analysis of the situation.
  • 策画万全 - Means 'planned for absolute safety,' indicating thorough preparation.
  • 甲妇与某生之命，固赖翁而生 - Means 'the wife of Mr. A and the life of Mr. B were indeed saved by the old man,' highlighting the old man's role in saving lives.
  • 即甲他日苟得再娶，而幸延一线之嗣者，亦出翁之赐也 - Means 'even if Mr. A were to remarry in the future and fortunately continue the family line, it would also be due to the old man's favor,' emphasizing the old man's influence.
  • 大抵遇此等事，谏劝阻止者少，撺掇怂恿者多 - Means 'generally, in such matters, those who advise, dissuade, or stop are few, while those who incite or encourage are many,' a general observation about human behavior.
  • 有损于人而无益于己 - Means 'harmful to others and beneficial to oneself,' describing a selfish and harmful attitude.
  • 不知是何居心也 - Means 'one cannot fathom what kind of heart this is,' expressing puzzlement at someone's motives.
  • 问有能不动声色、排难解纷，如翁之热肠为人、从容周密者乎 - Means 'is there anyone who can resolve difficulties and disputes without raising a fuss, like the old man's warm-hearted and meticulous nature?' Praises the old man's ability to handle problems calmly and effectively.
  • 予尝谓：人心即天心也，顺天者昌 - Means 'I have often said: human heart is the heart of heaven, and those who follow heaven will prosper,' a philosophical statement linking human actions to divine will.
  • 观翁后日，父子两受其报，天心不大可见哉 - Means 'seeing how the old man and his son both received their rewards in the future, can the heart of heaven not be clearly seen?' Suggests that the old man's good deeds were rewarded by heaven.

  • 公年登大耋 - Formal expression meaning 'the old gentleman had reached the age of eighty,' using the classical term *大耋* to denote advanced age.

  • 易箦时 - Refers to the moment of death, as *易箦* is a euphemism for the change of the mat on which one lies, traditionally associated with the dying.
  • 辛伯叩问是事 - Means 'Xin Bo asked about this matter,' where *叩问* is a respectful way of asking a question.
  • 此盛德事，吾何能为？ - A rhetorical question meaning 'This is a great virtuous deed, how could I do it?' *盛德* refers to great virtue or moral excellence.
  • 大抵乡人以我平日迂方，或拟议及之耳 - Means 'The villagers probably thought of me as old-fashioned in daily life, so they might have speculated about it.' *迂方* means old-fashioned or out of touch with modern ways.
  • 予与辛伯交最昵 - Means 'I was closest friends with Xin Bo,' where *昵* indicates a close, intimate relationship.
  • 观余公已事 - Means 'Seeing what happened to the old gentleman,' where *已事* refers to past events or outcomes.
  • 造物试验贤者 - Means 'Heaven tests the virtuous,' using the concept of *造物* (Heaven or the Creator) to suggest divine testing.
  • 索逋者 - Refers to a moneylender or debt collector, *逋* meaning a debt or obligation.
  • 必须钱三千 - Means 'needed three thousand coins,' where *必须* emphasizes necessity.
  • 暗中计 - Means 'in secret calculations,' *暗中* implying hidden or private actions.
  • 短褕之值恰以相抵 - Means 'the value of the short jacket just matched the amount,' where *相抵* means to offset or match.
  • 少一钱不可，多一钱亦不可 - Means 'one coin less is unacceptable, one coin more is also unacceptable,' emphasizing strict precision.
  • 在凡人处此 - Means 'for an ordinary person in this situation,' *凡人* referring to an average or common person.
  • 竟如公慨然持赠，空拳而归 - Means 'the old gentleman gave it away generously and returned empty-handed,' where *慨然* conveys a sense of generosity and *空拳* means returning with nothing.
  • 直行所无事乎 - Means 'as if nothing had happened,' *直行* suggesting a straightforward or unremarkable action.
  • 夫人闻之，绝无怨言，反以笑语相慰 - Means 'the lady heard about it and had no complaints, instead comforting him with laughter and words,' *绝无* emphasizing the absence of something.
  • 行阴德事，不使人知 - Means 'performing good deeds in secret,' *阴德* referring to hidden or unseen virtue.

  • 然不敢不勉也 - Means 'yet I dare not not strive,' indicating a commitment to personal improvement despite limitations.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 里乘子 - A literary device used to introduce a moral or philosophical reflection, often in classical Chinese texts.
  • 二公 - Refers to two individuals mentioned earlier in the text, likely virtuous and fortunate figures.
  • 造物 - Refers to the Creator or the forces of nature, a common theme in classical Chinese philosophy and literature.
  • 符節 - Refers to seals and their impressions, used metaphorically to describe a perfect fit or alignment.
  • 德涼福薄 - Refers to having little virtue and little fortune, a common self-assessment in classical Chinese literature.
  • 勉 - Refers to striving or making an effort, often used in the context of personal improvement.

  • 诸医涎其赏 - Means to drool or covet, meaning 'the doctors coveted the reward.'

  • 唇疔最毒难治 - Medical observation, meaning 'lip carbuncles are the most toxic and difficult to treat.'
  • 遍稽古书 - Means to search through ancient books, meaning 'to search through ancient books.'
  • 皆无此症 - Indicates the rarity of the illness, meaning 'there is no record of such a condition.'
  • 群谢无能 - Indicates a collective admission of failure, meaning 'they all admitted their inability to help.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘子曰 - Classical attribution formula meaning 'Li Chengzi said,' indicating a statement or commentary by Li Chengzi.
  • 翁年已逾曰艾 - Classical phrase meaning 'the old man has surpassed the age of sixty,' where '艾' (ai) traditionally refers to the age of sixty.
  • 自念无嗣 - Means 'he thought to himself that he has no heir,' expressing a personal reflection.
  • 何需多金 - Means 'why does one need much wealth,' questioning the value of excessive wealth.
  • 是天牖其衷也 - Means 'this is heaven opening his heart,' suggesting divine guidance or inspiration.
  • 其矢行善事 - Means 'he sincerely performs good deeds,' emphasizing the genuineness of his actions.
  • 难；其不求人知，尤难 - Means 'difficult; even more difficult is not seeking recognition from others,' highlighting the challenges of selfless good deeds.
  • 盖初存心固施不望报 - Means 'initially, he sincerely performs good deeds without expecting a reward,' explaining his initial motivation.
  • 而天必报之 - Means 'but heaven will certainly reward him,' indicating divine recompense.
  • 彼苍苍者，何尝丝毫负人哉 - Means 'Heaven, how could it ever fail anyone,' affirming the reliability of divine justice.
  • 观此 - Means 'considering this,' leading to a conclusion based on the preceding narrative.
  • 而天下之为富翁者，闻此翁之风，亦可以兴矣 - Means 'but those who are wealthy throughout the world, hearing of this old man's example, can also be inspired,' suggesting a positive influence.

  • 天牖其衷 - A metaphorical expression suggesting divine inspiration or guidance.
  • 矢行善事 - Refers to performing good deeds with sincerity and commitment.

  • 彼苍苍者 - Refers to Heaven, often personified in classical Chinese texts.
  • 为富不仁 - A common saying criticizing the idea that wealth leads to unkindness.
  • 阳虎 - A historical figure known for his controversial views and actions during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 富翁 - Refers to wealthy individuals, often used in classical texts to discuss wealth and morality.

  • 干怒冥王 - '干怒' means 'to anger,' and '冥王' refers to the ruler of the underworld.
  • 镌禄秩二级 - '镌' means 'to engrave' or 'to reduce,' '禄秩' refers to one's official rank and salary, and '二级' means 'two levels.'
  • 减寿二纪 - '减寿' means 'to shorten life,' and '二纪' refers to 24 years (a '纪' is 12 years).
  • 荡检益甚 - '荡检' means 'to abandon restraint,' and '益甚' means 'even more.'

  • 冥王以积恶多端 - '冥王' is the ruler of the underworld, '积恶' means 'accumulated evil,' and '多端' means 'in many ways.'
  • 罚仅以布衣终 - '罚' means 'punishment,' '仅' means 'only,' '布衣' refers to a commoner or ordinary person, and '终' means 'to end.'
  • 子孙亦不能显达 - '子孙' means 'descendants,' '亦' means 'also,' '不能' means 'cannot,' and '显达' means 'to become prominent or successful.'
  • 是真不可以救药矣 - '是真' means 'truly,' '不可以' means 'cannot,' '救药' means 'to be cured,' and '矣' is a particle indicating completion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 布衣 - A term for a commoner or ordinary person, often used in contrast to the nobility or officials.
  • 积恶多端 - A phrase indicating the accumulation of many sins or wrongdoings, often used in moral or religious contexts.

  • 距榻尺有咫 - 'About a foot away from the bed,' indicating the distance.
  • 一物从地出半身 - 'An object emerged from the ground halfway,' describing the appearance of the creature.
  • 长尺许 - 'About a foot long,' describing the size of the creature.
  • 黄毛毧毧 - 'With yellow, fluffy fur,' describing the appearance of the creature.
  • 状类弥猴 - 'Resembling a monkey,' comparing the creature to a monkey.
  • 掉头望榻上 - 'Turned its head to look at the bed,' describing the creature's action.
  • 气咻咻然 - 'Breathing heavily,' describing the creature's breathing.
  • 目碧色，炯如猫睛 - 'Its eyes were green and bright like a cat's,' describing the creature's eyes.
  • 歘遁不见 - 'Suddenly disappeared,' describing the creature's sudden vanishing.
  • 大骇 - 'Greatly frightened,' expressing the narrator's intense fear.
  • 以为目昏瞀，所见不确 - 'Thinking that my eyes were blurred and what I saw was not clear,' explaining the narrator's initial doubt.
  • 起挑灯 - 'Got up to relight the lamp,' describing the narrator's action.
  • 再就枕 - 'Lay down again,' indicating the narrator's attempt to sleep.
  • 心烦躁 - 'Feeling restless and agitated,' describing the narrator's emotional state.
  • 姑启目觇之 - 'Cautiously opened my eyes to peek,' describing the narrator's cautious observation.
  • 灯复暗 - 'The light became dim again,' describing the recurrence of the dim light.
  • 前物复自地出 - 'The previous object emerged from the ground again,' indicating the reappearance of the creature.
  • 依旧遁去 - 'Disappeared as before,' describing the creature's repeated vanishing.
  • 如是者三 - 'This happened three times,' indicating the frequency of the events.
  • 灯光益暗 - 'The light became even dimmer,' describing the worsening condition of the light.
  • 前物出地益高 - 'The previous object emerged from the ground even higher,' indicating the creature's increased height.
  • 不敢复就枕 - 'Dared not lie down again,' describing the narrator's fear.
  • 起呼仆叩僧门 - 'Got up and called the servant to knock on the monk's door,' describing the narrator's action.
  • 告以所见 - 'Told them what I had seen,' explaining the narrator's communication.
  • 僧点首笑曰 - 'The monk nodded and smiled, saying,' describing the monk's reaction.
  • 良有之 - 'Indeed, there is such a thing,' acknowledging the existence of the creature.
  • 老衲以君文星 - 'Old monk, you are a scholar,' addressing the narrator respectfully.
  • 当无患 - 'Should have no worry,' expressing reassurance.
  • 不虞其亦敢乃尔也 - 'Did not expect it to be so bold,' expressing surprise at the creature's behavior.
  • 爰为移榻别室 - 'Moved the bed to another room,' describing the monk's action.
  • 方得安寝 - 'Finally managed to sleep peacefully,' indicating the resolution of the problem.
  • 越数岁 - 'After several years,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 闻姑孰人言 - 'Hearing people from Gushu say,' indicating the source of information.
  • 前室地下故有枯棺 - 'There was an old coffin underground in the previous room,' providing additional context.
  • 年久成僵尸 - 'Which had turned into a zombie after many years,' describing the state of the coffin.
  • 今已掘去矣 - 'It has now been dug up,' indicating the removal of the coffin.
  • 幸未睡熟 - 'Fortunately, I was not fully asleep,' expressing relief.
  • 且未灭灯 - 'And the lamp was not extinguished,' emphasizing the importance of keeping the light on.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 家大人 - A respectful term for one's father, often used in Classical Chinese to denote a father's teachings.
  • 客游 - Refers to traveling as a guest, often associated with scholars and literati.
  • 生地 - Unfamiliar or dangerous places, often used to describe remote or unexplored areas.
  • 僮仆 - Servants, often used to denote lower-class individuals who serve the family.
  • 灯 - A traditional oil lamp, commonly used in ancient China for lighting.
  • 姑孰 - A historical place name, likely referring to a specific town or region.
  • 庵 - A small temple or hermitage, often used by monks or recluses.
  • 僵尸 - A zombie or reanimated corpse, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 枯棺 - An old, dried-out coffin, often associated with ancient burial practices.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 友见征君发髩、须眉、满头皆墨 - A vivid description of the 'friend' seeing the 'reclusive scholar' with his hair, beard, and eyebrows all blackened.

  • 不能强事丹青 - Means 'unable to force himself to engage in painting,' indicating reluctance or inability.
  • 急取水为之浣濯 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'quickly took water to wash it off,' suggesting an attempt to remove the blackness.
  • 遣人送归 - Means 'sent someone to send him back home,' indicating a formal act of returning.
  • 所画废纸上惟浓墨一团 - A literal description of the painting being just a 'mass of thick ink' on discarded paper.
  • 大于釜口 - A comparative expression meaning 'larger than the mouth of a pot,' emphasizing the size of the ink mass.
  • 不忍捐弃 - Means 'unwilling to discard,' indicating a sense of value or sentiment.
  • 姑叠折庋诸架上 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'temporarily folded and placed on a shelf,' indicating a temporary storage.
  • 一夜，天阴月黑 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'one night, the sky was dark and the moon was obscured,' setting a somber atmosphere.
  • 室内隐约放光 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'a faint light was emitted indoors,' indicating a mysterious phenomenon.
  • 始悟征君画果通神 - Means 'finally realized that the reclusive scholar's painting truly was divine,' indicating a moment of enlightenment.
  • 可惜败兴中辍 - Means 'it was a pity that it was interrupted in a disappointing manner,' indicating regret.
  • 未竟厥事也 - Means 'the matter was not completed,' indicating an unfinished task.
  • 京师打钟庵募修落成 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'the Bell Temple in the capital was rebuilt and completed,' indicating a significant event.
  • 僧慕征君名 - Means 'the monk admired the reclusive scholar's name,' indicating respect or admiration.
  • 丐书庵额 - Means 'asked to write the name of the temple,' indicating a request.
  • 以僧无行 - Means 'because the monk had no virtue,' indicating a negative judgment.

  • 僧稔某甲与征君善 - Means 'the monk knew that someone named Jia was friends with the reclusive scholar,' indicating a connection.
  • 啖以重金 - Means 'offered a large sum of money,' indicating bribery.
  • 求为转乞 - Means 'asked to be interceded,' indicating a plea.
  • 甲知征君为人 - Means 'Jia knew the reclusive scholar's character,' indicating familiarity.
  • 不敢遽达 - Means 'did not dare to immediately convey,' indicating caution.
  • 又虑无以报僧 - Means 'also worried about how to repay the monk,' indicating concern.
  • 既思得一法 - Means 'after thinking of a method,' indicating a plan.
  • 乃沽佳醖 - Means 'then bought good wine,' indicating a gesture.
  • 招征君饮 - Means 'invited the reclusive scholar to drink,' indicating a social interaction.
  • 又预作五绝诗一首 - Means 'also prepared a five-line poem in advance,' indicating preparation.
  • 将“打钟庵”三字嵌于诗中 - Means 'embedded the three characters 'Bell Temple' in the poem,' indicating a clever inclusion.
  • 乘征君微醺 - Means 'when the reclusive scholar was slightly drunk,' indicating a state of mind.
  • 自握笔书此诗 - Means 'wrote the poem himself,' indicating personal involvement.
  • 屡书屡自拉弃之 - Means 'wrote it repeatedly and then tore it up each time,' indicating frustration or perfectionism.
  • 征君睨之而笑 - Means 'the reclusive scholar glanced at it and laughed,' indicating a reaction.
  • 甲曰：“家有屏，欲书此诗刻其上，顾不善涂鸦，致贻君笑。” - Means 'Jia said, 'I have a screen at home and want to write this poem on it, but I'm not good at painting, which caused you to laugh,' indicating an explanation.
  • 时征君已醉 - Means 'at that time, the reclusive scholar was already drunk,' indicating a state of intoxication.
  • 笑曰：“我为汝代笔如何？” - Means 'laughed and said, 'How about I write it for you?'' indicating an offer.
  • 甲喜曰：“本不敢相烦，果尔，幸甚！” - Means 'Jia happily said, 'I didn't dare to trouble you, but if you do, it would be very fortunate!'' indicating gratitude.
  • 征君遽索纸 - Means 'the reclusive scholar quickly asked for paper,' indicating urgency.
  • 纵笔为之一挥 - Means 'wrote it in one stroke,' indicating skill and confidence.
  • 较常尤胜 - Means 'it was even better than usual,' indicating a high quality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 征君 - Refers to a reclusive scholar or hermit, often a person of high moral standing who has withdrawn from society.
  • 打钟庵 - Refers to the Bell Temple, a specific temple in the capital city.
  • 五绝诗 - Refers to a type of classical Chinese poetry consisting of five lines, each with five or seven characters.
  • 酒 - Refers to alcohol, a common social lubricant in classical Chinese culture.
  • 屏 - Refers to a screen or panel, often used for decoration or calligraphy in traditional Chinese homes.
  • 涂鸦 - Refers to scribbling or drawing, often used to describe poor-quality artwork.
  • 代笔 - Refers to someone writing or drawing on behalf of another, often used in a context of assistance or favor.

Proper Nouns

  • 打钟庵 - The Bell Temple.
  • 五绝诗 - The five-line poem.
  • 甲 - The person named Jia.
  • 僧 - The monk.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甲請曰 - 甲 is a person's name or pseudonym, and 請曰 means 'requested to say' or 'asked.'
  • 既蒙賜書 - 賜書 means 'to receive a letter' or 'to be given a letter,' and 既蒙 indicates that the speaker has already received the letter.
  • 即求署款 - 署款 means 'to sign one's name' or 'to affix one's signature.'
  • 以為蓬舍光 - 蓬舍 is a humble term for one's home, and 光 means 'to shine' or 'to bring honor.'
  • 尤深欣感 - 尤深 means 'especially deep,' and 欣感 means 'joyful gratitude' or 'deep appreciation.'
  • 征君笑而許之 - 征君 refers to a reclusive scholar or hermit, and 笑而許之 means 'smiled and agreed.'
  • 后甲刓此三字授僧，榜于门 - 刓 means 'to carve' or 'to inscribe,' and 榜于门 means 'posted on the door.'
  • 征君偶过庵前 - 偶过 means 'happened to pass by,' and 庵 refers to a small temple or hermitage.
  • 讶额署己款 - 讶 means 'surprised,' and 署己款 means 'signed with one's own name.'
  • 注视沈思良久 - 注视 means 'stared at,' 沈思 means 'deeply thought,' and 良久 means 'for a long time.'
  • 忽忆前为甲书屏中有此三字 - 忽忆 means 'suddenly remembered,' 书屏 means 'wrote on a screen' or 'inscribed on a screen,' and 有此三字 means 'these three characters.'
  • 始悟为甲所卖，遂与绝交 - 始悟 means 'finally realized,' 为甲所卖 means 'was deceived by 甲,' and 遂与绝交 means 'and thus severed ties.'
  • 征君精医 - 精医 means 'excellent in medicine' or 'skilled in medicine.'
  • 今所传世者，仅妇科书 - 今所传世者 means 'what is passed down today,' 仅 means 'only,' and 妇科书 means 'books on gynecology.'
  • 顾不徒精妇科也 - 顾 means 'but,' 不徒 means 'not just,' and 精妇科也 means 'skilled in gynecology.'
  • 有同乡某客都中 - 同乡 means 'fellow townsman,' 某 is a placeholder name, 客 means 'visitor' or 'guest,' and 都中 means 'in the capital.'
  • 忽患头痛 - 忽患 means 'suddenly suffered from,' and 头痛 means 'headache.'
  • 经多医无效 - 经多医 means 'after seeing many doctors,' and 无效 means 'ineffective.'
  • 闻太医院某公为国手 - 闻 means 'heard,' 太医院 means 'Imperial Medical Academy,' 某公 is a placeholder name, and 为国手 means 'is a national expert.'
  • 特造请诊视 - 特造 means 'especially went,' 请 means 'to request,' and 诊视 means 'to diagnose and treat.'
  • 公按脉毕，命之曰 - 公 refers to the doctor, 按脉 means 'took the pulse,' 毕 means 'finished,' and 命之曰 means 'told him.'
  • 此一月症也 - 此 means 'this,' 一月 means 'one month,' and 症 means 'symptom' or 'illness.'
  • 可速归家料理后事，迟无及矣 - 可速 means 'can quickly,' 归家 means 'return home,' 料理后事 means 'arrange for the afterlife,' and 迟无及矣 means 'it will be too late if delayed.'
  • 某闻，怏怏归寓 - 某闻 means 'the man heard,' 怏怏 means 'discontentedly,' and 归寓 means 'returned to his lodging.'
  • 急治任，兼程旋里 - 急治任 means 'hurriedly prepared his belongings,' 兼程 means 'at a faster pace,' and 旋里 means 'returned home.'
  • 会征君入都，遇诸途 - 会 means 'happened,' 征君入都 means 'the reclusive scholar entered the capital,' and 遇诸途 means 'met on the road.'
  • 问某归意，以疾告 - 问某归意 means 'asked about the man's intention to return,' and 以疾告 means 'informed about his illness.'
  • 太医院某公，今国手也 - 太医院某公 is repeated, and 今国手也 means 'is a national expert today.'
  • 盍请治之 - 盍 means 'why not,' and 请治之 means 'ask for treatment.'
  • 某叹曰 - 某叹曰 means 'the man sighed and said.'
  • 仆此归，正遵某公命也 - 仆 means 'I,' 此归 means 'this return,' 正遵 means 'following,' and 某公命也 means 'the doctor's command.'
  • 乃具告所言 - 乃 means 'then,' 具告 means 'fully informed,' and 所言 means 'what was said.'
  • 征君曰 - 征君曰 means 'the reclusive scholar sighed and said.'
  • 果尔，奈何 - 果尔 means 'if so,' and 奈何 means 'what to do.'
  • 我试为汝诊之 - 我试为汝诊之 means 'I will try to diagnose it for you.'
  • 按脉良久，叹曰 - 按脉良久 means 'took the pulse for a long time,' and 叹曰 means 'sighed and said.'
  • 某公真国手也 - 某公真国手也 means 'the doctor is truly a national expert.'
  • 其言不谬 - 其言 means 'his words,' and 不谬 means 'are not mistaken.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 征君 - Refers to a reclusive scholar or hermit, often associated with a life of seclusion and moral integrity.
  • 太医院 - Refers to the Imperial Medical Academy, a prestigious institution in ancient China responsible for the health of the imperial family and officials.
  • 国手 - A term used to describe a national expert or master in a particular field, often in the context of medicine, chess, or other specialized arts.
  • 兼程 - A term indicating that someone is traveling at a faster pace, often to make up for lost time.
  • 仆 - A humble term used by a servant or a lower-ranking person when addressing a superior.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 某固知征君技不在某公下 - Formal expression meaning 'I firmly know that the Master's skill is not inferior to that of Master Gong.'
  • 泫然泣曰 - Vivid description of weeping, meaning 'with tears streaming down his face, he said.'
  • 诚如君言，某真无生望矣 - Means 'If what you say is true, I truly have no hope of survival.'
  • 然君久著和缓名 - Means 'However, you have long been renowned for your healing skills.'
  • 竟不能生死人而肉白骨乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Could you not bring the dead back to life and make white bones flesh?'
  • 征君又沈思久之 - Means 'The Master pondered deeply for a long time.'
  • 汝疾万无生理 - Means 'Your illness has no chance of recovery.'
  • 今思得一法，愈则不任功；不愈，亦不任过 - Means 'I have now thought of a method. If you recover, I cannot claim credit; if you do not, I cannot be blamed.'
  • 汝如法试之何如 - Means 'How about you try this method?'
  • 某大喜，求方 - Means 'I was greatly pleased and asked for the prescription.'
  • 征君命归家，遍觅健少所着旧毡笠十余枚 - Means 'The Master instructed me to go home and collect more than ten old felt hats worn by strong young men.'
  • 煎浓汤，漉成膏，旦夕服之，当有效 - Means 'Boil them into a thick decoction, strain it into a paste, and take it morning and evening. It should be effective.'
  • 万一幸愈，可速至都中谒某公，当云何也 - Means 'If you are fortunate enough to recover, you should quickly go to the capital to see Master Gong and say what I have told you.'
  • 某谢诺而别，归家如法治之，疾果瘥 - Means 'I thanked him and left, then followed his instructions at home, and my illness indeed recovered.'
  • 寻至都中，见征君，喜慰异常，趣往谒某公 - Means 'Soon, I went to the capital to see the Master, who was extremely happy, and hurried to see Master Gong.'
  • 公见某至，瞿然曰 - Means 'When Master Gong saw me, he was startled and said.'
  • 君犹无恙耶 - Means 'You are still unharmed?'
  • 某具以征君所治之法告之 - Means 'I told him in detail about the method the Master used to treat me.'
  • 傅君神医，吾不及也 - Means 'Master Fu is a divine physician; I cannot match him.'
  • 吾初诊汝疾，系脑髓亏耗 - Means 'When I first diagnosed your illness, it was due to a depletion of brain marrow.'
  • 按古方，惟生人脑可疗，顾万不能致 - Means 'According to ancient prescriptions, only fresh human brain could treat it, but it is impossible to obtain.'
  • 今傅君以健少旧毡笠多枚代之，真神医 - Means 'Now Master Fu has used many old felt hats worn by strong young men as a substitute. He is truly a divine physician.'
  • 吾尚敢业此哉 - Means 'How dare I continue in this profession!'
  • 公送某出，即乞休，闭门谢客，绝口不谈医矣 - Means 'Master Gong saw me out, then immediately retired, shut himself in, refused visitors, and never spoke of medicine again.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 征君 - Refers to a respected and highly skilled physician.
  • 某公 - Refers to another respected physician, likely a contemporary or peer of the Master.
  • 脑髓亏耗 - A medical term referring to a depletion of brain marrow, which was considered a serious condition in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 生人脑 - Refers to fresh human brain, which was believed to be a potent remedy for certain conditions in ancient Chinese medicine, though it is not a practice that would be acceptable today.

Proper Nouns

  • 征君 - The respected physician.
  • 某公 - Another respected physician.
  • 傅君 - The respected physician who treated the patient.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 里乘卷二 - Title of the text, where '里乘' (lǐchéng) refers to a collection of stories or essays, and '卷二' (juǎn èr) indicates it is the second volume.
  • 山右陈翁 - '山右' (shānyòu) refers to Shanxi Province, and '陈翁' (Chén Wēng) means 'Mr. Chen.' This phrase introduces the main character, an elderly man from Shanxi.
  • 相国文贞公祖也 - '相国' (xiàngguó) refers to a high-ranking official, and '文贞公' (Wénzhēng Gōng) is a posthumous title given to a person after death, often indicating a noble or respected status. '祖也' (zǔ yě) means 'his ancestor.' This phrase provides background information about Mr. Chen’s lineage.
  • 家贫，以舌耕为业 - '家贫' (jiā pín) means 'poor family,' and '以舌耕为业' (yǐ shé gēng wéi yè) is an idiom meaning 'to make a living by teaching or speaking.' '舌耕' (shé gēng) literally means 'tilling with one’s tongue,' symbolizing intellectual labor.
  • 年至不惑 - '年至不惑' (nián zhì bù huò) means 'in his forties,' derived from the Confucian concept of '不惑' (bù huò), which refers to the age when one no longer feels confused or uncertain.
  • 计累岁所积修金共四五十金 - '计累岁所积修金' (jì lěi suì suǒ jī xiū jīn) means 'the total savings accumulated over the years,' and '四五十金' (sì wǔ shí jīn) means 'forty to fifty taels of gold.' '修金' (xiū jīn) refers to savings or wealth.
  • 镕小铤十余枚 - '镕' (róng) means 'to melt,' and '小铤' (xiǎo tǐng) refers to small ingots of gold. '十余枚' (shí yú méi) means 'more than ten.'
  • 一一摩挲 - '一一' (yī yī) means 'one by one,' and '摩挲' (mó suō) means 'to stroke or fondle.' This phrase describes the action of handling the gold ingots carefully.
  • 聊以自娱 - '聊以自娱' (liáo yǐ zì yú) means 'to amuse oneself' or 'to take pleasure in something simple.'
  • 媪尝戏而揶揄之 - '媪' (ǎo) refers to an elderly woman, likely the wife of Mr. Chen. '尝戏而揶揄之' (cháng xì ér yé yú zhī) means 'once teased him playfully.'
  • 不以为侮也 - '不以为侮也' (bù yǐ wéi wǔ yě) means 'did not take it as an insult.'
  • 村有族子某 - '村' (cūn) means 'village,' and '族子某' (zú zǐ mǒu) means 'a distant relative from the village.' The name '某' (mǒu) is a placeholder for an unknown individual.
  • 困阨无以自存 - '困阨' (kùn è) means 'in dire straits,' and '无以自存' (wú yǐ zì cún) means 'unable to support himself.'
  • 素知翁所积 - '素知' (sù zhī) means 'has always known,' and '翁所积' (wēng suǒ jī) means 'what Mr. Chen has saved.'
  • 尝伏窗窥之 - '尝伏窗窥之' (cháng fú chuāng kuī zhī) means 'once peered through the window.'
  • 欲胠箧窃取 - '欲' (yù) means 'wanted,' '胠箧' (qū qiè) means 'to open a chest or box,' and '窃取' (qiè qǔ) means 'to steal.'
  • 苦无间 - '苦无间' (kǔ wú jiàn) means 'found no opportunity.'
  • 一夜，媪启户如厕 - '一夜' (yī yè) means 'one night,' '媪启户' (ǎo qǐ hù) means 'the elderly woman opened the door,' and '如厕' (rú cè) means 'went to the bathroom.'
  • 乘间潜入 - '乘间' (chéng jiàn) means 'took advantage of the opportunity,' and '潜入' (qián rù) means 'slipped in secretly.'
  • 稔知积金固置褥下 - '稔知' (rěn zhī) means 'well aware,' '积金' (jī jīn) means 'saved gold,' and '固置褥下' (gù zhì rù xià) means 'firmly placed under the mattress.'
  • 急探手暗中摸索 - '急探手' (jí tàn shǒu) means 'quickly reached out,' and '暗中摸索' (àn zhōng mō suǒ) means 'felt around in the dark.'
  • 翁睡固未熟 - '翁睡' (wēng shuì) means 'Mr. Chen was sleeping,' and '固未熟' (gù wèi shú) means 'not yet fully asleep.'
  • 觉而擥其袂 - '觉' (jué) means 'woke up,' and '擥其袂' (lǎn qí mèi) means 'grabbed his sleeve.'
  • 就炕炉取火烛之 - '就炕炉' (jiù kàng lú) means 'went to the stove,' '取火' (qǔ huǒ) means 'lit a fire,' and '烛之' (zhú zhī) means 'illuminated it.'
  • 见是某 - '见是某' (jiàn shì mǒu) means 'saw it was someone.'
  • 大惊 - '大惊' (dà jīng) means 'greatly startled.'
  • 仍息其烛 - '仍息其烛' (réng xī qí zhú) means 'extinguished the light again.'
  • 低声问曰 - '低声问曰' (dī shēng wèn yuē) means 'asked in a low voice.'
  • 汝胡为者 - '汝胡为者' (rǔ hú wéi zhě) means 'what are you doing?'
  • 奈何作此丑事 - '奈何作此丑事' (nài hé zuò cǐ chǒu shì) means 'why do such a disgraceful thing?'
  • 为宗族羞 - '为宗族羞' (wéi zōng zú xiū) means 'bring shame to the family.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 山右 - Refers to Shanxi Province, historically known as 'Shanxi' or 'Shanyou.' This term is used to indicate the region where Mr. Chen comes from.
  • 相国文贞公 - A posthumous title given to a person of high rank and virtue. In this context, it indicates that Mr. Chen is a descendant of a respected official.
  • 家贫，以舌耕为业 - The idiom '以舌耕为业' (yǐ shé gēng wéi yè) reflects the cultural value placed on intellectual work and teaching, even in times of poverty.
  • 年至不惑 - The phrase '不惑' (bù huò) is derived from the Confucian concept of '四十而不惑' (sìshí ér bù huò), which means 'at forty, one no longer feels confused or uncertain.' This reflects the Confucian ideal of personal development and wisdom.
  • 计累岁所积修金 - The concept of saving and accumulating wealth over time is a common theme in Chinese literature, reflecting the importance of frugality and long-term planning.
  • 镕小铤十余枚 - The practice of melting and shaping gold into small ingots is a traditional method of storing wealth, reflecting the cultural significance of gold in Chinese society.
  • 一一摩挲 - The act of handling precious items with care is a common theme in Chinese literature, symbolizing the value placed on material possessions and the emotional attachment to them.
  • 聊以自娱 - The idea of finding joy in simple pleasures is a recurring theme in Chinese literature, reflecting the cultural emphasis on contentment and inner peace.

Idiomatic Expressions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妾不幸枉死 - Formal expression meaning 'I, the concubine, was unjustly killed.'
  • 命也 - Literally 'fate,' used to express acceptance of a tragic event.
  • 于君何尤 - Means 'what fault do I have with you?' indicating that the concubine sees her death as beyond the man's control.
  • 稽君冥籍 - Means 'to consult your records in the netherworld,' referring to the concubine's knowledge of the man's afterlife records.
  • 前程远大 - Means 'a great future ahead,' referring to the man's prospects.
  • 感君爱妾私意 - Means 'moved by your private affection for me,' expressing the concubine's understanding of the man's feelings.
  • 特来预贺 - Means 'to specially come to congratulate in advance,' indicating the concubine's supernatural intervention.
  • 今科必中魁选 - Means 'you will definitely pass the top examination this year,' referring to the man's academic success.
  • 首艺中权遣词尚未妥适 - Means 'the central part of your first essay is not yet properly phrased,' indicating a specific area needing improvement.
  • 重加改订 - Means 'to revise thoroughly,' suggesting a detailed review and correction.
  • 斟酌数字 - Means 'to carefully consider and adjust the wording,' indicating a meticulous approach to editing.
  • 得之矣 - Means 'I have it now,' expressing the concubine's satisfaction with the revised essay.
  • 二三场好自为之 - Means 'do well in the next few exams,' offering encouragement for future success.
  • 但求无弊 - Means 'just avoid any mistakes,' emphasizing the importance of careful preparation.
  • 高枕坦卧 - Means 'sleep soundly and peacefully,' indicating a state of confidence and relaxation.
  • 静听捷音 - Means 'wait quietly for good news,' expressing the expectation of positive results.
  • 生怅然神痴者久之 - Means 'the man was deeply melancholic and lost in thought for a long time,' describing his emotional state.
  • 深感女意 - Means 'deeply felt the concubine's intentions,' expressing the man's gratitude and appreciation.
  • 自悔前事孟浪 - Means 'regretted his previous impulsive actions,' indicating a sense of remorse.
  • 薄情太甚 - Means 'too cold-hearted,' expressing the man's self-criticism.
  • 无可如何矣 - Means 'there is nothing to be done about it,' indicating resignation.
  • 揭晓 - Means 'the results were announced,' referring to the publication of the exam results.
  • 果中经魁 - Means 'indeed passed the top examination,' confirming the man's success.
  • 罢官家居 - Means 'retired from office and lived at home,' describing the man's father's current status.
  • 闻生闱中事 - Means 'heard about the son's examination,' indicating the father's awareness of the son's academic endeavors.
  • 窃幸女不念旧恶 - Means 'secretly hoped that the woman did not hold onto old grudges,' expressing the father's relief.
  • 既得捷报 - Means 'having received the good news,' referring to the announcement of the son's success.
  • 老怀愈增快慰 - Means 'the old man's heart was even more joyful,' describing the father's happiness.
  • 贺客阏门 - Means 'guests came to congratulate at the door,' indicating the celebration.
  • 乐不可支 - Means 'could not contain his joy,' expressing the father's overwhelming happiness.
  • 献岁即趣子束装 - Means 'at the beginning of the new year, he urged his son to pack his bags,' indicating the father's encouragement for the son to prepare for the next step.
  • 计偕北上 - Means 'planned to go north together,' referring to the son's journey to the imperial capital.
  • 指日可作老封君矣 - Means 'could soon become an honored father-in-law,' expressing the father's hope for the son's future success.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妾 - Refers to a concubine or secondary wife, indicating a lower social status compared to a primary wife.
  • 枉死 - Refers to an unjust or premature death, often associated with supernatural or tragic circumstances.
  • 冥籍 - Refers to the records of the netherworld, a concept in Chinese folklore and religion.
  • 魁选 - Refers to passing the top examination, a significant achievement in the imperial examination system.
  • 首艺 - Refers to the first essay in the imperial examination, a critical component of the test.
  • 二三场 - Refers to the subsequent exams in the imperial examination process.
  • 高枕坦卧 - A metaphorical expression meaning to sleep soundly, indicating a state of confidence and peace of mind.
  • 静听捷音 - A metaphorical expression meaning to wait quietly for good news, indicating anticipation and hope.
  • 罢官家居 - Refers to retirement from official duties and living at home, a common practice for officials who had served their term.
  • 老封君 - Refers to an honored father-in-law, a title given to a man whose daughter has married into a prestigious family.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sentence '妾不幸枉死，命也，于君何尤？' must be read as a single unit, expressing the concubine's acceptance of her fate and her lack of blame towards the man.
  • The long sequence '稽君冥籍，前程远大，感君爱妾私意，特来预贺，今科必中魁选。' must be read as a single unit, describing the concubine's supernatural intervention and her belief in the man's future success.
  • The clause '惟首艺中权遣词尚未妥适，必如此修饰，方为完璧。' must be read as a single unit, indicating the specific area needing improvement and the necessary revisions.
  • The clause '生细思之，良然，果如女言，重加改订。' must be read as a single unit, describing the man's reflection and the subsequent revisions.
  • The clause '女并为斟酌数字，始称尽美尽善。' must be read as a single unit, indicating the concubine's final approval of the revised essay.
  • The clause '女笑曰：“得之矣。二三场好自为之，但求无弊，便可高枕坦卧，静听捷音。妾去矣。”' must be read as a single unit, containing the concubine's final words of encouragement and her departure.
  • The short clause '歘然遽逝。' must be read as a single unit, describing the concubine's sudden disappearance.
  • The long sequence '生怅然神痴者久之，深感女意，又自悔前事孟浪，未免薄情太甚，然已无可如何矣。' must be read as a single unit, describing the man's emotional state and his reflections.
  • The clause '及揭晓，果中经魁。' must be read as a single unit, confirming the man's success in the examination.
  • The clause '时生父已罢官家居，闻生闱中事，窃幸女不念旧恶；既得捷报，老怀愈增快慰。' must be read as a single unit, describing the father's reaction to the son's success.
  • The clause '贺客阏门，乐不可支。' must be read as a single unit, describing the celebration.
  • The clause '献岁即趣子束装，计偕北上，以谓指日可作老封君矣。' must be read as a single unit, describing the father's encouragement for the son to prepare for the next step.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃翁日晡假寐胡床 - Literal translation: 'Your father napped on a trundle bed in the afternoon.' '假寐' (nap) suggests a light sleep or doze.
  • 倏见女子披发伸舌来前 - Literal translation: 'Suddenly, a woman with disheveled hair and protruding tongue appeared before him.' '披发' (disheveled hair) and '伸舌' (protruding tongue) are symbolic of a vengeful spirit.
  • 怒容满面，戟手指而詈之曰 - Literal translation: 'With a full face of anger, she pointed her finger like a spear and scolded him.' '戟指' (pointing like a spear) is a metaphor for a threatening gesture.
  • 老贼尚在此梦梦耶 - Literal translation: 'Old thief, are you still dreaming?' '梦梦' (dreaming) is used sarcastically to imply ignorance or foolishness.
  • 汝子乘人之危，无礼于妾 - Literal translation: 'Your son took advantage of my misfortune and was disrespectful to me.' '乘人之危' (taking advantage of someone's misfortune) is a common idiom.
  • 致死非命 - Literal translation: 'Caused my death by unnatural means.' '非命' (unnatural death) implies a violent or untimely death.
  • 汝初既失义方之教，后又无悔过之心 - Literal translation: 'You failed to teach him right from wrong, and later had no remorse.' '义方之教' (teaching right from wrong) is a key Confucian concept.
  • 犹痴顽无耻，妄想作老封君耶 - Literal translation: 'Still foolish and shameless, you foolishly think you can become an old official?' '老封君' (old official) refers to a retired or respected official.
  • 妾在闱中，非不能索汝子之命 - Literal translation: 'I in the inner chambers, am not powerless to take your son's life.' '闱中' (inner chambers) refers to the private quarters of a woman.
  • 然等闲措大，犹不足以大伤厥考心 - Literal translation: 'But an ordinary scholar is not enough to deeply wound your father's heart.' '等闲措大' (ordinary scholar) is a term for a common scholar.
  • 然后妾乃得而甘心焉，则厥考之心伤矣 - Literal translation: 'Only then can I take satisfaction, and your father's heart will be deeply wounded.' '甘心' (take satisfaction) and '厥考之心' (your father's heart) are key phrases.
  • 实告老贼，汝仅此子，妾稽其冥籍，秩居极品，寿臻期颐 - Literal translation: 'I tell you, your son is your only child. I checked his fate in the afterlife records, and he is destined for the highest rank and to live to a hundred.' '冥籍' (afterlife records), '极品' (highest rank), and '期颐' (a hundred years) are key terms.
  • 妾请命于帝，已罚削殆尽 - Literal translation: 'I have petitioned the emperor, and his fate has been almost entirely erased.' '罚削殆尽' (almost entirely erased) implies a severe punishment.
  • 许妾于芦沟桥旅次报仇泄忿矣 - Literal translation: 'I have been allowed to take revenge at the inn on the Lugou Bridge.' '芦沟桥' (Lugou Bridge) is a specific historical location.
  • 汝尚梦梦，妄想作老封君耶 - Literal translation: 'You still dream, foolishly thinking you can become an old official?' '梦梦' (dreaming) is used sarcastically again.
  • 遽前批其颊曰 - Literal translation: 'She suddenly slapped his cheek and said.' '批其颊' (slap his cheek) is a physical act of reprimand.
  • 汝如不信，不日当有好音至矣 - Literal translation: 'If you don't believe me, good news will come soon.' '好音' (good news) is a euphemism for death.
  • 其父惊觉，知其不祥 - Literal translation: 'His father woke up in shock, realizing the bad omen.' '不祥' (bad omen) implies impending doom.
  • 亟遣使兼程追生归 - Literal translation: 'He immediately sent a messenger to return home at top speed.' '兼程' (at top speed) implies urgency.
  • 使至中途，见傔从已扶生柩南旋 - Literal translation: 'The messenger reached halfway and saw the servants carrying the coffin southward.' '傔从' (servants) and '生柩' (coffin) are key terms.
  • 果于芦沟桥旅次自经云 - Literal translation: 'Indeed, he had hanged himself at the inn on the Lugou Bridge.' '自经' (hanged himself) is a euphemism for suicide.
  • 其嗣以斩 - Literal translation: 'His descendant was executed.' '以斩' (executed) is a legal term indicating capital punishment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乃翁 - Refers to the father of the deceased son.
  • 胡床 - A type of folding chair or stool used in ancient China.
  • 芦沟桥 - A famous bridge in Beijing, historically significant.
  • 冥籍 - The afterlife records, a concept in Chinese cosmology.
  • 极品 - The highest rank in the imperial hierarchy.
  • 期颐 - A hundred years, a symbol of longevity.
  • 老封君 - An old or retired official, a term of respect.
  • 措大 - A common scholar, often used in a derogatory sense.
  • 好音 - A euphemism for death, often used in classical Chinese.
  • 不祥 - A bad omen, often associated with impending disaster.
  • 兼程 - At top speed, indicating urgency.
  • 傔从 - Servants or attendants.
  • 生柩 - The coffin of the deceased.
  • 自经 - A euphemism for suicide, often used in classical Chinese.
  • 以斩 - Executed, a legal term indicating capital punishment.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卿傎也耶 - Colloquial expression meaning 'Are you crazy?' or 'Are you out of your mind?' used to express surprise or disbelief.
  • 明知小生读书未成，何从求仕 - Direct question meaning 'You know I haven't finished my studies, how can I seek office?'
  • 司马文园尚不以货郎为耻 - Reference to Sima Xiangru, a famous Han dynasty writer, who did not consider being a peddler shameful. Implies that even great men can engage in humble occupations.
  • 古之循良吏皆由科目起家耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Did all ancient virtuous and upright officials start their careers through the imperial examination system?'
  • 君如有仕志，曷不纳资以求之 - Suggestion meaning 'If you have ambitions for office, why don't you pay a fee to obtain it?'
  • 些须薄资，仅敷糊口，那有多资可纳 - Statement meaning 'My meager savings are just enough to make ends meet, how can I have extra money to pay a fee?'
  • 卿益复呓语 - Colloquial expression meaning 'You're even more delusional' or 'You're talking nonsense.'
  • 些须薄资 - Phrase meaning 'meager savings' or 'a little money.'
  • 纳资以求之 - Term meaning 'pay a fee to obtain it,' referring to the practice of buying an official position.
  • 佐贰 - Refers to a deputy official.
  • 丞倅、牧令 - Refers to county magistrates and other local officials.
  • 万不得已，亦必须如此 - Phrase meaning 'If absolutely necessary, it must be done this way.'
  • 庶可稍展妾志 - Statement meaning 'It would allow me to somewhat fulfill my aspirations.'
  • 此中所藏，不止一郡守之资 - Statement meaning 'The contents of this robe are not just enough to buy the position of a prefect.'
  • 其余可留作资斧，无俟他求也 - Statement meaning 'The rest can be kept as funds, without needing to seek elsewhere.'
  • 以两仪刀折之，中缀明珠不下三千颗 - Description meaning 'Using a two-yin knife, I cut it open to reveal at least three thousand pearls.'
  • 坎第鬻之，共得五万余金 - Statement meaning 'Selling them in batches, I obtained over fifty thousand taels of gold.'
  • 遂罢贾，趣生赴部纳资，铨得粤东太守，指日挈眷之任 - Sequence of actions meaning 'I then stopped trading, urged the young man to go to the Ministry to pay the fee, and he was appointed as the prefect of Guangdong, ready to take up his post with his family soon.'

  • 变产作孤注 - A metaphorical expression meaning to risk everything in one final gamble, derived from the gambling term '孤注' (betting everything on one throw).
  • 图恢扩旧业 - Means 'to attempt to expand the old business or estate,' where '恢扩' refers to expansion or enlargement.
  • 其计已左 - Means 'his plan is already wrong' or 'his strategy is flawed,' where '左' implies deviation or error.
  • 二万金挥霍殆尽 - Literally means 'two million gold coins were spent almost entirely,' referring to extravagant spending.
  • 脱非遇姬，亦沟中瘠而已 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'if not for meeting Ji, he would have been nothing but a dead body in a ditch,' where '沟中瘠' refers to a corpse in a ditch, symbolizing failure or ruin.
  • 贸易少安便无他志 - Means 'once he settled into trade, he had no other ambitions,' where '少安' implies settling into a stable situation.

  • 庸夫偏有此庸福 - Means 'an ordinary man has this ordinary good fortune,' where '庸福' implies ordinary or mediocre luck.
  • 伊谁之力哉 - A rhetorical question meaning 'who is the force behind this?'
  • 仙露作为 - Refers to the actions or deeds of '仙露,' likely a character in the text, possibly a woman or a figure of supernatural or extraordinary qualities.
  • 事事令人可爱、可法 - Means 'everything about her is lovable and worthy of emulation,' where '可法' implies being a model or worthy of imitation.
  • 初属其求仕，继佐其为政，终促其勇退 - Means 'at first she helped him seek office, then assisted him in governance, and finally encouraged him to retire bravely,' where '勇退' implies retiring with courage or dignity.
  • 且劝力行种种善事 - Means 'and she also encouraged him to actively perform various good deeds,' where '力行' implies active and earnest effort.
  • 乡人称颂 - Means 'the villagers praised her,' where '称颂' implies praise or commendation.

  • 多非须眉所能及 - Means 'most men cannot reach her level,' where '须眉' is a metaphor for men.
  • 何况巾帼而勾栏者 - Means 'let alone women from the entertainment quarters,' where '巾帼' refers to women, and '勾栏' refers to the entertainment quarters or brothels.
  • 噫嘻！异矣！ - An interjection expressing surprise or astonishment, meaning 'Ah! How extraordinary!'
